questions.question: Why are primitive types like int or char forbidden from being passed directly as parameters to Java Generics?
answers: Primitives consume too much L1 cache memory causing thread scheduler stalls
Generics require object references on the heap; primitives do not inherit from Object
Database drivers cannot bind primitive types to statement query parameters
The modularity guidelines written in module-info block primitive parameters

questions.question: What primary objective does Java's Type Erasure accomplish when compiling generic code architectures?
answers: It restricts collection instances from scaling horizontally
It converts object reference addresses into encrypted byte arrays
It forces the compiler to duplicate class files for every unique type
It replaces type parameters with bounds or Object to ensure type safety without runtime overhead

questions.question: What behavioral trait separates a TreeMap from a standard HashMap implementation?
answers: TreeMap is limited to storing integer primitive values
TreeMap maintains its keys sorted according to their natural ordering or a custom Comparator
HashMap encrypts key fields; TreeMap leaves characters visible
TreeMap prevents asynchronous thread execution steps from accessing data

questions.question: Which interface inside the Java Collections Framework guarantees that no duplicate elements can be stored?
answers: Map entries tracker
List
Set
Queue

questions.question: How does a HashMap handle key value lookups efficiently under the hood?
answers: It computes the key's hashCode() to locate a bucket array slot containing data node links
It writes the object properties out to temporary text files
It references a foreign key constraint row mapped from a local tracker
It runs a full linear search loop across all entries sequentially

questions.question: What is a major performance difference between an ArrayList and a LinkedList in Java?
answers: ArrayList resizes dynamically without duplicating its internal index arrays
LinkedList consumes less memory because it drops link node pointer sets
ArrayList provides O(1) random access time; LinkedList provides O(1) element insertion at boundaries
LinkedList supports horizontal clustering distributions

questions.question: What restriction applies to a class declared with the final modifier keyword in Java?
answers: The internal variables are rendered immutable values instantly
The class cannot be extended or subclassed by any other class structure
The JVM forces code tasks to run inside kernel mode
The class cannot contain any public instance initialization constructors

questions.question: What does a class inherit when it implements a Java Interface containing default methods?
answers: The concrete private instance fields
Complete access to the abstract module's private memory
The interface's default functional implementations without breaking backward compatibility
Automated thread synchronization parameters across loop executions

questions.question: Which pillar of OOP allows a subclass to provide a specific implementation of a method defined in its superclass?
answers: Polymorphism (Method Overriding)
Abstraction execution rules
Inheritance (Method Overloading)
Encapsulation

questions.question: What separates method overriding from method overloading in Java programming execution?
answers: Overriding replaces a superclass method at runtime; overloading uses distinct parameters at compile-time
Overloading is exclusive to abstract interfaces
Overloading forces garbage collection routines
Overriding modifies class access modifiers; overloading alters return type bounds
